semanticCore: Goring with horns, head butting, colliding, animal killed by head attack, or astronomical mansion
primaryMeaning: Goring with horns/animal killed by butting

keyDerivations.meaning: Animal gored to death by another's horns, head-butted corpse

figurativeOrRareUsages: حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ وَالدَّمُ ... وَالنَّطِيحَةُ وَمَا أَكَلَ السَّبُعُ (5:3): Forbidden to you is dead meat, blood... and that killed by goring/butting, and what has been partially eaten by predators.
summaryEn: The root refers to head-butting or horn-goring (Nath), listing the gored animal corpse (Nateeha) as part of the dietary prohibitions for health and ritual purity.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى صدم الحيوان بقرنه وضربه برأسه وموت الشاة نطحاً (نطح)، محرماً أكل الشاة النطيحة التي ماتت بفعل نطح شاة أخرى لها لعدم التذكية.
rawText: انتطحتِ الكِبَاشُ 8 , and 🡻تناطحت, (S, K,) The rams smote one another with their horns. (K.) === لَا يَنْطِحُ فِيهَا عَنْزَانِ [Two she-goats will not, in it, smite each other with their horns]: i. e., two weak persons will not encounter each other in it: alluding to a case in which there will not happen any discord or contention. (L, from a trad.) تَنَاْطَحَ 6 see 8. === تَنَاطَحَتِ الأَمْوَاجُ, and السُّيُولُ, ‡ [The waves, and the torrents, conflicted, or dashed together.] (A.) دَائِرَةُ النَّاطِحِ A certain circular or spiral curl of hair on a horse, which is deemed unlucky. (L.) [See نَطِيحٌ.] === نَاطِحٌ ‡ A difficulty; a distressing event; an affliction; a calamity: pl. نَوَاطِحُ. (S, K.) Ex. أَصَابَهُ نَاطِحٌ A difficulty, or distressing event, befell him. (S.) نَوَاطِحُ الدَّهْرِ The difficulties, &c., of fortune. (S.) === النَّطْحُ وَالنَّاطِحُ ‡ [The two stars called] الشَّرَطَانِ, [q. v.,] which are [the two stars in] the two horns of Aries: (S, K:) [the first of the Mansions of the Moon: the latter is b, and the former a:] ISd says, that 🡻النَّطْحُ is a star which is one of the Mansions of the Moon, and from [the auroral rising of] which one augurs evil: and IAar says, that one says, نَطْحٌ and النَّطْحُ, without and with ال; and the like in the case of the name of any of the Mansions of the Moon. (TA.) The Arabs said إِذَا طَلَعَ النَّطْحُ طَابَ السَّطْحُ ‡ [When En-Nat-h rises aurorally, the house-top becomes pleasant as a place on which to sit in the evening, or on which to sleep]. (A.) === مَا لَهُ نَاطِحٌ وَلَا خَابِطٌ He has neither sheep, or goat, nor camel. (S, K.) كَبْشٌ نَطَّاحٌ [A ram that smites much with his horn]: (S:) and 🡻نَطُوحٌ [signifies the same: and] is applied to a man [app. as meaning who pushes, thrusts, or repels, much, or vehemently]. (IAar, TA in art. ردس.) نَاطَحَا 3 , inf. n. نِطَاحٌ (L, Msb) and مُنَاطَحَةٌ, (Msb,) They two (rams or he-goats) smote each other with their horns. (L.) === بَيْنَهُمَا نِطَاحٌ ‡ [Between them two is a contention like that of two rams]; said of two learned men, and of two merchants. (A.) === جَرَى لَنَا فِى السُّوقِ نِطَاحٌ ‡ [There happened to us in the market a contention like that of two rams]. (A.) === [نَاطَحَهُ He, or it, faced, or was or came opposite to him, or it]: نِطَاحٌ is syn. with مُقَابَلَةٌ in the dial. of El-Hijáz. (TA.) نَطَحَهُ 1 , aor. ﹻ and ﹷ, (S, K,) inf. n. نَطْحٌ, (S,) He (a ram, S, L, and the like, L) smote him with his horn. (K.) === مَا نَطَحَتْ فِيهِ جَمَّآءَ ذَاتُ قَرْنٍ [A horned animal did not smite with its horn for him a hornless ewe]: a proverb, said of him who has perished unavenged. (L.) [See also Freytag's Arab. Prov., ii. 507.] === نَطَحَهُ عَنْهُ ‡ He pushed him, or thrust him, away from him, and removed him. (A.) نَطُوحٌ : see نَطَّاحٌ. نَطِيحٌ Smitten with the horn and so killed: fem. with ة: (L, K:) pl. نَطْحَى (L) and نَطَائِحُ: (Lh, L:) you say also نَعْجَةٌ نَطِيحٌ, as well as نطيحة. (L.) === Also نَطِيحَةٌ A sheep or goat (Az) smitten with the horn and so killed. (Az, S,) and of which the flesh is therefore unlawful to be eaten: (Az:) the ة is added because it is made a subst., not an epithet: (Az:) or because the quality of a subst. predominates in it, as in the case of فَرِيسَةٌ and أَكِيلَةٌ and رَمِيَّةٌ. (S.) === نَطِيحٌ and 🡻نَاطِحٌ ‡ What comes to thee from before thee, (or faces thee, L,) of birds and wild animals (S, L, K) &c., which one rouses by throwing a stone or by crying out, and from the course of which one augurs evil or good: (L:) contr. of قَعِيدٌ. (S.) [The نطيح or 🡻ناطح is of evil omen.] === Hence, (TA,) نَطِيحٌ ‡ An unfortunate, or unlucky, man. (K.) === نَطِيحٌ A horse having a blaze, or white mark on his face, extending to one of his ears, which is deemed unlucky: (L:) or a horse having on his forehead two circular or spiral curls of hair (دَائِرَتَانِ), which are disliked: (S, L, K:) if there be but one, it is called دَائِرَةُ اللَّطَاةِ; and this is not disliked; (S;) or it is called لَطْمَةٌ; and the horse, لَطِيمٌ. (L.)
